# Product Requirements Document

## Overview

Summarize the user outcome and why it matters.

## Product Context

Reference the product brief, mission, product thesis, market gap, and lane ownership.

## Linked Brief

Link the product brief that this PRD sharpens. If no brief exists, explain why the work can safely start at the PRD layer.

## User Problem

Describe the current pain clearly, including any fragmentation tax, category fatigue, shame cycle, reimbursement distortion, or forward-looking clarity gap involved.

## Goals

List the user and product goals.

## Non-Goals

List what this PRD explicitly does not cover.

## Requirements

List functional, UX, privacy, and operational requirements.

## UX and Behavior Expectations

Describe the intended user experience, tone, and decision-support behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

List the concrete conditions that must be true before implementation can be considered correct.

## Trust and Risk Constraints

Document security, privacy, auth, shared-visibility, billing, or compliance constraints.

## Success Metrics

List the signals that would indicate success or failure.

## Rollout and Release Notes

Record the likely release gate, dependencies, and launch constraints.

## Open Decisions

Record unresolved issues with recommendation, rationale, and what would change the decision.
